changes_summary: Corrected root-cause analysis (removed _reconcile_orphaned_tasks as a competing path — it only handles dead workers). Preserved failsafe startup_phase guard via new `in_startup_phase` field on DerivedWorkerState. Narrowed health check cleanup to actual duplicates (stale+queued at lines 539-547) and dead code (no-heartbeat branches at 521-528, 556-562), explicitly keeping legitimate async checks (idle timeout, failure streaks, basic health). Dropped Phase 2 (mixin wiring) as out of scope.
questions: Is there a CI pipeline or specific test command to run? I see tests/ but want to confirm the right invocation (pytest, specific markers, etc).

The _perform_basic_health_check at line 549-553 (fresh heartbeat but not claiming, with queued tasks) applies a 60s threshold that's tighter than gpu_idle_timeout_sec. Should this tighter check eventually move into derive_worker_state as a separate termination condition, or is it intentionally a supplementary runtime probe?
success_criteria: DerivedWorkerState has `in_startup_phase: bool` field that is set regardless of heartbeat status
`_failsafe_stale_worker_check` accepts `List[DerivedWorkerState]` and does zero raw datetime parsing or metadata access
`_health_check_active_workers` has no duplicate stale+queued check — the only verdict branch is `ws.should_terminate` at the top
Dead code removed: no-heartbeat branches in health check (lines 521-528, 556-562) are gone
startup_phase raw metadata access (`metadata.get('startup_phase')`) does not appear in `_failsafe_stale_worker_check`
Idle-timeout-with-no-work path (lines 564-573) is preserved unchanged in health phase
New tests cover: in_startup_phase field derivation (with and without heartbeat), failsafe skipping startup workers
assumptions: derive_worker_state() remains a pure synchronous function — async DB queries (failure streaks, task history, idle timeout) stay in the health phase.

_perform_basic_health_check (heartbeat < 60s) is a tighter threshold than the stale detection in derive_worker_state (gpu_idle_timeout_sec). It stays in the health phase as a supplementary probe for the fresh-but-not-claiming case.
The control/phases/ mixin files are stale copies that should be updated to match canonical code but NOT wired in as part of this plan.
_reconcile_orphaned_tasks is NOT a health verdict path and requires no changes in this plan — it correctly only handles ERROR/TERMINATED workers.

The idle-timeout-with-no-work path (lines 564-573) using _is_worker_idle_with_timeout is a scale-down concern, not a health verdict. It stays in the health phase and is NOT mapped to any derived field.
structure_warnings: Each step section should include at least one numbered substep.
